reasoning: Tyrobp (DAP12) functions as an immunoreceptor‑signalling adaptor primarily in myeloid cells, where it couples receptors such as TREM2 to downstream kinases and modulates inflammatory pathways (e.g., NF‑κB, MAPK). Selenop encodes the secreted selenium‑transport protein selenoprotein P, whose transcription is mainly controlled by oxidative‑stress regulators such as NRF2 and by systemic selenium status. There is no established mechanistic link between Tyrobp‑mediated immune signalling and the transcriptional control of Selenop. Consequently, CRISPRi‑mediated knockdown of Tyrobp is unlikely to cause a consistent up‑ or down‑regulation of Selenop expression.
